title: Use offline production readiness proxies before runtime go-live checks
context: Runtime deploy checks require a real production environment, but the repository still needs PR-time evidence that API contracts, request body validation, schema migrations, and production config surfaces are not drifting silently.
decision: Add .ai/production-readiness.json and pnpm ai:production as mandatory machine gates. The gate records backend API route methods, body-reading route/helper validation status, model schema fingerprints, migration files, schema snapshots, production env template coverage, and actual env placeholder checks for go-live runs.
consequences: New or changed backend API methods fail until the public route contract snapshot is updated.
Routes or API helpers that read request bodies fail unless they have validateAndTransformBody middleware coverage or an expiring accepted debt entry.
PRs cannot add new accepted request-body validation debt against the base commit.
Model schema fingerprints are checked against registered migrations and snapshots; PRs changing fingerprints must add migration files.

Production env templates must include all required production config keys, while actual production env files are rejected if they contain empty, example, localhost, or replace-with placeholder values.
Runtime deploy health, edge, regression, backup, and rollback evidence are still required before go-live; this ADR only adds CI-safe offline proxies.
